tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, low CO₂, and significant proteinuria, suggesting impaired renal function. Urine findings (glucosuria, ketonuria, bilirubinuria, low specific gravity, brown color, and ammoniacal odor) indicate possible tubular dysfunction and metabolic derangement. Despite normal serum glucose, low HbA1c may reflect anemia or recent changes in glycemic control. Recommend further renal evaluation and assessment for underlying metabolic or hepatic disorders.

display.value: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
